en.extract:
The Samoan fantail (Rhipidura nebulosa) is a species of bird in the family Rhipiduridae.
It is endemic to Samoa and is found in Upolu and Savaiʻi islands.


Description
The plumage is mostly dark greyish-brown with white undertail, black tail and light (white or pale smoky) throat. White spot behind the eyes. Like other fantails, Samoan fantail often spreads its long tail like a fan. The usual sounds are a low chirp. Sometimes imitates the voices of other bird species.


Distribution and habitat
The Samoan fantail is described as common and widespread on Upolu Island and has a limited distribution on the island of Savaiʻi.
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests. Prefers primary and secondary rainforests, but tolerates man-made landscapes: plantations and gardens.


Taxonomy
According to IOC there are 2 recognised subspecies. In alphabetical order, these are:

R. n. altera Mayr, 1931
R. n. nebulosa Peale, 1849
Samoan fantail (R. nebulosa) forms a superspecies with: 

Brown fantail (R. drownei)
Makira fantail (R. tenebrosa)
Rennell fantail (R. rennelliana)
Streaked fantail (R. verreauxi)
Kadavu fantail (R. personata)

The '''Samoan fantail''' ('''''Rhipidura nebulosa''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Rhipiduridae]].
It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Samoa]] and is found in [[Upolu]] and [[Savaiʻi]] islands.

== Description ==
The plumage is mostly dark greyish-brown with white undertail, black tail and light (white or pale smoky) throat. White spot behind the eyes. Like other fantails, Samoan fantail often spreads its long tail like a fan. The usual sounds are a low chirp. Sometimes imitates the voices of other bird species.<ref name="eBird">{{Cite web|title=Samoan Fantail|url=https://ebird.org/species/samfan1/|language=en|website=Ebird}}: photos, description, range map</ref>

== Distribution and habitat ==
The Samoan fantail is described as common and widespread on [[Upolu]] Island and has a limited distribution on the island of [[Savaiʻi]].<ref name="Hoyo">del Hoyo, J.; Elliott, A.; Christie, D. 2006. Handbook of the Birds of the World, vol. 11: Old World Flycatchers to Old World Warblers. Lynx Edicions, Barcelona, Spain. (cit. by {{cite iucn |author=BirdLife International |date=2016 |title=''Rhipidura nebulosa'' |volume=2016 |article-number=e.T22706878A94094907 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2016-3.RLTS.T22706878A94094907.en}})</ref>

Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s. Prefers primary and secondary rainforests, but tolerates man-made landscapes: plantations and gardens.<ref name="eBird"/>

== Taxonomy ==

According to [[International Ornithologists' Union|IOC]] there are 2 recognised subspecies.<ref name=IOC>Gill F., Donsker D. & Rasmussen P. (Eds.): [https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/orioles/ Orioles, drongos, fantails]. ''[[International Ornithologists' Union|IOC]] World Bird List (v11.2)''. doi:10.14344/IOC.ML.11.2</ref> In alphabetical order, these are:
* ''R. n. altera'' Mayr, 1931
* '''''R. n. nebulosa'' Peale, 1849'''

Samoan fantail (''R. nebulosa'') forms a [[superspecies]] with:<ref name="ngb">{{cite web |author  = Tvardikova K. |url = https://pngbirds.myspecies.info/content/rhipidura-drownei  |title = ''Rhipidura drownei'' Gould, 1843 |language = en |website = New Guinea Birds online |publisher   = |date =2022|access-date  = 2022-02-24|ref=New Guinea Birds online}}</ref> 
* [[Brown fantail]] (''R. drownei'')
* [[Makira fantail]] (''R. tenebrosa'')
* [[Rennell fantail]] (''R. rennelliana'')
* [[Streaked fantail]] (''R. verreauxi'')
* [[Kadavu fantail]] (''R. personata'')
